evals.name: cart-store-optimistic-with-request-status
evals.prompt: I'm building an Angular 19 e-commerce frontend. I need a CartStore using @ngrx/signals that holds the user's cart items, exposes a total and item count, supports add/remove/clear, and uses optimistic updates with rollback when calling our CartApiService (it has add(item), remove(id), clear() — all returning Promises that may reject). The store should be app-wide (providedIn root). Also surface request status so the UI can show 'saving…' and error states. Generate the full TypeScript implementation: CartStore, the CartApiService interface, and the with-request-status custom feature. Also add a small Vitest/Jest test that verifies optimistic add works and that a rejected add rolls back the cart.
evals.expected_output: A CartStore using signalStore with withState (CartState typed), withComputed (total + count), withMethods that call inject(CartApiService), uses patchState with getState snapshot for rollback, includes withRequestStatus custom feature with setPending/setFulfilled/setError standalone updaters. Tests use TestBed.inject and a mocked service.
expectations: Imports come from '@ngrx/signals' (not '@ngrx/store')
Declares an explicit CartState type passed to withState<CartState>
Uses signalStore with { providedIn: 'root' } config
Uses patchState (never direct .set on signals from outside)
HTTP / persistence is in a separate service injected via inject(), NOT inlined in withMethods using HttpClient
Implements optimistic update by snapshotting state with getState before the call and restoring it on the catch branch
Exposes a withRequestStatus custom feature defined via signalStoreFeature with setPending/setFulfilled/setError standalone updater functions
Computed total and count are defined inside withComputed (not as plain methods)
Test file uses TestBed.inject(CartStore) and overrides CartApiService via { provide: CartApiService, useValue: ... }
Test asserts that an add followed by a rejected promise restores the previous cart contents

evals.name: refactor-behaviorsubject-to-signal-store
evals.expected_output: Two files: ProductsApiService (just HTTP) and ProductsStore (signalStore with state, computed inStockProducts, loadAll method, add method). Component example uses store.products() and store.inStockProducts() in the template, drops async pipe.
expectations: Splits the service into a ProductsApiService that owns HttpClient AND a ProductsStore that owns state
ProductsStore uses signalStore from '@ngrx/signals' with { providedIn: 'root' }
BehaviorSubjects are replaced by withState<ProductsState>({ products: [], isLoading: false }) — explicitly typed
inStockProducts is a withComputed (not a method, not piped through anything)
loadAll uses patchState for both the loading flag and the result, and calls the injected ProductsApiService
Add method uses patchState with a function-form updater (not direct mutation)
Component snippet calls store.products() and store.inStockProducts() as signals (no | async pipe)
ProductsStore does NOT import HttpClient directly

evals.name: custom-feature-with-selected-entity
evals.prompt: I have a BooksStore using `@ngrx/signals` with `withEntities<Book>()`. I want to add a reusable custom feature called `withSelectedEntity<T>()` that I can compose into any entity-based store. It should: (1) add a `selectedId` state slice, (2) expose a `selectedEntity` computed that returns the entity from `entityMap` for the current `selectedId` (or null), (3) add `select(id)` and `clearSelection()` methods, (4) be type-safe so it gives a compile error if I try to add it to a store without `withEntities`. Show me the full feature implementation, the updated BooksStore composing it, and a unit test that wraps the feature in a minimal signalStore and proves it works.
evals.expected_output: A withSelectedEntity factory function returning signalStoreFeature with the typed { state: type<EntityState<T>>() } prerequisite declaration. Updated BooksStore composing withEntities<Book>() then withSelectedEntity<Book>(). A test wrapping the feature in a fresh signalStore.
expectations: withSelectedEntity is implemented with signalStoreFeature from '@ngrx/signals'
The feature declares its prerequisite using the typed overload with { state: type<EntityState<T>>() } (or equivalent constraint) to require withEntities upstream
Adds a selectedId state slice with type EntityId | null
Exposes selectedEntity as a withComputed signal that reads from entityMap and returns null when selectedId is null
Provides select(id) and clearSelection() methods that use patchState
BooksStore composes withEntities<Book>() BEFORE withSelectedEntity<Book>() (order matters)
Test wraps the feature in a fresh signalStore (not the real BooksStore) and uses TestBed.inject
Test verifies select() updates selectedEntity and clearSelection() resets it to null

evals.name: rxmethod-debounced-search
evals.prompt: Build a `BookSearchStore` using @ngrx/signals for a typeahead search. State: `query: string`, `results: Book[]`, `isLoading: boolean`. The component will call `store.searchByQuery(query)` on every keystroke. Requirements: debounce keystrokes by 300ms, drop duplicates, cancel in-flight requests when a new keystroke comes in (so an old slow response can't overwrite a newer one), and handle errors without breaking the stream. The backend is a `BooksApi.search(query: string): Observable<Book[]>`. Use the right tool for the job and explain in a short comment why you used it. Provide the store and a quick test.
evals.expected_output: Store using rxMethod with pipe(debounceTime, distinctUntilChanged, switchMap, tapResponse). Component calls store.searchByQuery(query) and signals are read in the template. Test uses TestBed.inject + mocked BooksApi and asserts state updates after the debounce window.
expectations: Uses rxMethod from '@ngrx/signals/rxjs-interop'
Pipeline contains debounceTime(300), distinctUntilChanged, and switchMap (in that order or equivalent semantics)
Uses tapResponse (or equivalent error-isolating handling) so an inner error doesn't terminate the rxMethod stream
patchState updates isLoading before the HTTP call and resets it on both success and error paths
Does not use signalMethod for this case (RxJS operators required)
Includes a brief comment justifying the rxMethod choice over signalMethod / plain async
Test uses TestBed.inject(BookSearchStore), mocks BooksApi via providers, and asserts results after a wait/poll for the debounce

evals.name: todos-entities-store-crud
evals.prompt: Build a TodosStore using `@ngrx/signals/entities` for a normal todo list. Each Todo has `{ id: string; text: string; completed: boolean; createdAt: Date }`. Methods needed: addTodo(text), removeTodo(id), toggleCompleted(id), removeAllCompleted(), and loadAll() that fetches from a TodosApiService. Add computed signals for: total count, active count (not completed), and completed count. The store is app-wide. Include a `withRequestStatus` feature for the load. Show me the full store, the request-status feature, and a unit test that exercises addTodo + toggleCompleted + the computed signals.
evals.expected_output: TodosStore using withEntities<Todo>() + withRequestStatus(). Methods use addEntity, removeEntity, updateEntity, removeEntities (predicate), setAllEntities. Computed counts use the entities() signal. Tests cover add + toggle + counts.
expectations: Imports withEntities from '@ngrx/signals/entities' (not from '@ngrx/signals')
Uses entity updaters (addEntity, removeEntity, updateEntity) from '@ngrx/signals/entities' rather than hand-rolled array splicing
removeAllCompleted uses removeEntities with a predicate function (not a manual filter + setAllEntities)
toggleCompleted uses updateEntity with the function-form changes ((todo) => ({ completed: !todo.completed }))
loadAll uses setAllEntities together with setFulfilled / setPending request-status updaters
Computed counts use the entities() signal (or ids().length where appropriate) inside withComputed
withRequestStatus is implemented as signalStoreFeature in a separate function (reusable)
Test uses TestBed.inject(TodosStore), exercises addTodo + toggleCompleted, and asserts both the entities and the computed counts
